docket_entries.description: AMENDED PETITION FILED BY PETITIONER DEL PRADO, RENE AS TO RESPONDENT SOLON HOUSE, LLC DOES 1 THROUGH 10
MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-08-2023 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION IS OFF CALENDAR. INSUFFICIENT NOTICE. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE AMENDED PETITION DOES NOT PROVIDE ANY STATUTORY BASIS FOR A RELIEF DEMANDED VIA A PETITION PROCEDURE IN A CIVIL ACTION AS OPPOSED TO A COMPLAINT, WHICH WOULD REQUIRE AN ISSUANCE OF SUMMONS. IF PETITIONER RE-NOTICES THE PETITION FOR HEARING, THE STATUTORY AUTHORITY FOR THE PETITION PROCEEDING SHALL BE INCLUDED. FURTHERMORE, PETITIONER MUST PROPERLY SERVE THE RESPONDENT/DEFENDANT. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition
Off Calendar Aug-08-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER RENE DEL PRADO'S AMENDED PETITION TO COMPEL THE DEFENDANTS FOR THE RETURN OF PLAINTIFF'S LATE MOTHER'S URN, ALONG WITH OTHER PERSONAL PROPERTY, DELIBERATELY STOLEN OR DESTROYED BY DEFENDANTS

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Transferred to be heard in department 501 at 9:30 a.m. on the same day. That department handles all landlord-tenant/real property cases per SF Local Rule 8.10A1. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (302/RBU)

NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION
Off Calendar Aug-08-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Rene Del Prado 415-730-3177 plaintiff in pro per appearing via Zoom.
No other appearances.

Plaintiff does not contest but requests clarification from the court regarding tentative.
Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient Notice. The Court notes that the Amended Petition does not provide any statutory basis for a relief demanded via a petition procedure in a civil action as opposed to a complaint, which would require an issuance of summons. If petitioner re-notices the petition for hearing, the statutory authority for the petition proceeding shall be included. Furthermore, petitioner must properly serve the respondent/defendant.

Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)
